commit.message:
fix: CGTP Strategy 2 link scan always runs, not just as fallback

Featured/hero articles on CGTP's listing page sit outside the regular
article grid, so Strategy 1 misses them even when it finds other articles.
Strategy 2 (direct accao-e-luta link scan) now always supplements Strategy
1; seenUrls deduplication prevents any duplicates.

commit.message:
fix: CGTP listing parser falls back to direct <a> when no h2 link found

CGTP's Bootstrap 5 template uses bare <a> tags for some article links
(no h2/h3 wrapper), causing Strategy 1 to miss articles like the SEP
enfermeiros March 20 strike. Added fallback to any accao-e-luta/ link
within the article container so those articles are captured.

commit.message:
fix: correct stFileUploaderDropzone testid (typo was missing 'r')

All 5 wrong selectors used 'stFileUploadDropzone' — the real testid
in Streamlit 1.54.0's JS bundle is 'stFileUploaderDropzone' (with r).
Confirmed by inspecting index.C4KskYz6.js in the Streamlit package.
This was the root cause of the white file uploader background.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix type error: cast article as ArticleData in public article page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: CGTP crawler uses h2 for article title, not section h1

CGTP's Bootstrap 5 template puts the section header ("Acção e Luta")
in h1 and the actual article title in a plain h2. The crawler was
picking up the section h1, discarding the listing title, causing
employer and date extraction to fail.

- fetchArticleContent: try h2 selectors before h1
- normaliseCgtpArticle: only prefer article-page title when it's at
  least as long as the listing title (guards against short section headers)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase logo, text and navbar strip size for better visibility

navbar strip: 70→90px (desktop), 60→74px (mobile).

commit.message:
feat: sharp Claude Decisions redesign — max visibility, no scrolling

- All text white (removed all grey-fg)
- Header: ACTION  bar  conf%  ✓N on-chain  ~N dry run  ✗N failed  ↳ trigger
- Rationale: single line clipped at 74 chars (no word-wrap taking 3+ lines)
- Unified trade table: execution status inline per row (✓/~/✗/⚠/·)
  uses execution.trades when available, falls back to decision.trades
- Flags: dedup, max 4 shown + "+N more", critical (BLOCKED/HALT/STOP) in red
- Recent: last 6 decisions, clean time/action/conf/trigger columns
- Two thin white separator lines replace bulky cyan section headers
- Target: full decision visible in ~16 lines without scrolling

commit.message:
perf: skip redundant Secret Manager writes with token hash check

Add _last_saved_token_hash to track per-user token state and skip
_save_token_to_secret when access_token, refresh_token, and expires_at
haven't changed. Eliminates ~0.5s overhead per sap_query call when
the token was not refreshed during the query.

Also add Cloud Trace IAM permission guidance comment to deploy script
for enable_tracing=True.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: sap-gemini-enterprise
repository.description: sap-gemini-enterprise

commit.message:
fix(security): resolve 10 production audit findings

Critical:
- timing-safe comparison: replaced === with crypto.timingSafeEqual()
  in MetricsKeyGuard and AgentApiKeyGuard (prevents timing attacks)

Access control — added @Roles() to 5 unprotected controllers:
- cash-finance: owner, admin, manager
- trip-analytics: owner, admin, manager (+ PeriodQueryDto validation)
- vhm24-integration: owner, admin, manager
- investor dividends: owner, admin (+ organizationId tenant filter)
- metrics: owner, admin

Multi-tenant:
- promo-codes: duplicate check now scoped to organizationId (was global)
- investor findDividends: added organizationId filter

Data integrity:
- collections: replaced hard delete (.remove) with .softDelete()

Performance:
- Added @Index on organizationId: investor-profile, dividend-payment
- Added @Index on investorProfileId: dividend-payment
- Added @Index on organizationId: payment-refund

Infrastructure:
- Removed DB_USER/DB_PASSWORD from Grafana container environment
- Reverted ajv override (breaks ESLint 9.x internal ajv dependency)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2.3.4 - Optimize hash calculation and add transcode features

- Optimize hash calculation: only read head/middle/tail portions (64KB each) for files > 192KB
- Add two-stage upload response for real-time transcoding progress
- Fix localStorage quota errors with error handling
- Add original bitrate option for transcoding
- Fix ALAC codec detection (now transcoded to AAC)
- Add multi-language support for upload toasts
- Add startup scripts (restart.bat, stop.bat)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add production deployment setup with secure webhook payment verification

- Add Razorpay webhook handler with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification
- Add findByPaymentOrderId to EnrollmentRepository for webhook lookups
- Permit /api/payment/webhook in SecurityConfig (public, verified by signature)
- Add webhook-secret config to application.yml and application-prod.yml
- Rewrite docker-compose.prod.yml: add all missing env vars, uploads/logs volumes, backend internal-only, certbot auto-renewal
- Update nginx.conf: SSL termination, HTTP→HTTPS redirect, /api/ proxy to backend, security headers
- Update .gitignore: add target/, .env, uploads/, OS artifacts
- Update .env.example: add all required keys with documentation
- Add init-ssl.sh: one-time Let's Encrypt SSL setup script
- Add DEPLOYMENT.md: complete step-by-step deployment guide
- Untrack target/ compiled classes from git

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

PDF (report_template.html) :

  après la barre de score et avant les risques prioritaires

commit.message:
feat: CTA générique dans hero article blog

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

gh-69: reject variable reassignment at compile time (#73)

Calcium variables are immutable by design. This change enforces that
guarantee by emitting a compile-time error when a variable that is
already bound in the current scope is reassigned.

Changes:
- Add IsDefinedInCurrentScope method to SymbolTable
- Return CompileError on reassignment in AssignmentStatement
- Return CompileError on reassignment in ArrayDestructuringStatement
- Update csv/mod.test.ca and test/mod.test.ca to pipeline style
- Add TestReassignmentRejected to verify the new behavior
- Remove reassignment test case from TestGlobalAssignment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add EtherCAT Start SmartCell

Adds KinoEtherCAT.StartCell registered as "EtherCAT Start" in
Livebook's smart cell picker. Scans the bus via a temporary
EtherCAT.start/stop cycle, populates a slave table with discovered
station identities (VID/PID), and generates a formatted
EtherCAT.start/1 config using quoted AST + quoted_to_string/1.

- Application module registers the SmartCell on startup
- Slave name and driver fields are editable; driver is parsed via
  Code.string_to_quoted/1 with graceful fallback on invalid input
- Single configurable domain (id + cycle_time_us)
- State persisted in attrs; reloads in discovered state if slaves saved

commit.message:
fix(multi): chart of accounts entityId in claims, transmittal file inclusion

Root cause fixes for two long-standing bugs:

Chart of Accounts empty page:
- claims.entityId was never set because Cloud Function omitted it
- Added entityId to custom claims in onUserUpdate (default: 'default-entity')
- CoA page now uses 'default-entity' fallback matching other modules

Transmittal ZIP missing document files:
- submissionService.updateMasterDocument() never set lastSubmissionId
- GenerateTransmittalDialog couldn't find submissions to get file URLs
- Now sets lastSubmissionId and lastSubmissionDate on master doc update
- Added fallback: queries submissions by masterDocumentId when
  lastSubmissionId is missing (handles existing documents)

commit.message:
Fix canViewTicket: CX subDepartment is a list filter, not an access barrier

Before: A CX user with subDepartment="Customer Support" could not access
any ticket that had a vendorHandle, even if she needed to work on it.
This caused 403 on PATCH for Sadia and any CX agent with a subDepartment.

After: All CX users (any subDepartment) can VIEW and UPDATE any ticket
in the CX department. subDepartment still filters the default list view
(filterTicketsByDepartmentAccess) so agents see their relevant tickets
first — but it is no longer an access barrier on individual ticket ops.
